pattern: Build the bars with the widest possible strokes: extra operations are only ever needed where the target rises above the bar immediately to its left.
hints: Imagine building every bar one unit of height at a time using the widest strokes possible; a stroke can keep covering a run of positions as long as none of them dips below the current height.

Compare each element to the previous one (treat the position before the array as height zero): a rise of d over the previous height needs d additional strokes that were not required for the bars so far.
Sum every positive difference target[i] - target[i-1] across the array (with target[-1] treated as 0); that sum is the minimum number of operations.

edge_inputs.description: a plateau in the middle should not add extra operations
a single tall spike surrounded by low values

stress.description: moderate random heights across the full value range
